steps.title: Spot the passage's central focus
steps.content: The passage describes hagfish's slimy threads and then shifts to the key point: Negishi's team wants to recreate these threads in a lab as an eco-friendly alternative to petroleum-based fibers. The passage is ultimately about using hagfish threads to create new fabrics.

steps.title: Match the central idea to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly states the passage's main idea: the threads “might help researchers create a new kind of fabric.” This captures what Negishi's team is doing—recreating the threads as an eco-friendly alternative to petroleum fibers.

The other three either contradict this direction (B claims they're developing petroleum-based fabric, when the passage seeks a non-petroleum alternative), skip past the main point to address logistics (C focuses on why they don't farm hagfish, not what they're creating), or invent an unsupported relationship (D claims slime “compensates for” being jawless, which the passage doesn't state).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
